So the cursor selects a position in the bottom row (pink symbols). When I move LEFT/RIGHT, the cursor moves. When I move UP/DOWN, it probably changes the symbol at that position.
The cursor is currently at col=38, which corresponds to the 4th pink symbol (the one that changed to a 'P'-like shape after my earlier DOWN action).
Wait, let me re-examine. The white blocks are at col=45 (rightmost) initially, and after LEFT they moved to col=38. Let me figure out the positions of the pink symbols to understand which one the cursor is on.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Cursor (white blocks) moved from col=45 to col=38 with LEFT
# Need to map cursor positions to symbol positions

# Get pink symbol positions from current frame
after = last_transition.after_frame
pinks = [n for n in after.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'P']

# Group by row to find the bottom row of pink symbols
from collections import defaultdict
row_groups = defaultdict(list)
for p in pinks:
    center_row = sum(pt[0] for pt in p['boundary']) / len(p['boundary'])
    center_col = sum(pt[1] for pt in p['boundary']) / len(p['boundary'])
    row_groups[round(center_row)].append((center_col, p['hash'][:8]))

print("Pink symbol groups by row:")
for row in sorted(row_groups.keys()):
    cols = sorted(row_groups[row], key=lambda x: x[0])
    print(f"  Row {row}: {len(cols)} symbols at cols {[c[0] for c in cols]}")

# White cursor positions
whites = [n for n in after.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W']
print("\nWhite cursor positions:")
for w in whites:
    cr = sum(pt[0] for pt in w['boundary']) / len(w['boundary'])
    cc = sum(pt[1] for pt in w['boundary']) / len(w['boundary'])
    print(f"  row={cr:.1f}, col={cc:.1f}, hash={w['hash'][:8]}, pixels={w['pixels']}")

# Also check blue row symbols
blues = [n for n in after.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'S']
print("\nBlue symbol positions:")
for b in blues:
    cr = sum(pt[0] for pt in b['boundary']) / len(b['boundary'])
    cc = sum(pt[1] for pt in b['boundary']) / len(b['boundary'])
    print(f"  row={cr:.1f}, col={cc:.1f}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
